title: Billing Payments
body:
Invoices are paid by ACH transfer or credit card. The billing system sends monthly invoice reminders, records payment confirmation, and retries failed card payments. Customers can update payment methods before an invoice is due.

Payment operations also mention refunds, credits, account balance, failed payment retries, and billing contacts. This section repeats invoice payment ACH card billing refund credit balance terms so the document spans multiple chunks while preserving the relevant payment section.

Billing operations paragraph one: invoice payment ACH card billing refund credit balance confirmation monthly payment method retry invoice due account billing contact card ACH payment invoice. Billing operations paragraph two: invoice payment ACH card billing refund credit balance confirmation monthly payment method retry invoice due account billing contact card ACH payment invoice. Decoy words: shipping customs webhook encryption pagination export authentication. These words appear only to make retrieval choose the billing evidence instead of relying on isolated keywords.

title: Refunds And Returns
body: Refund processing starts after the returns center receives a parcel. Refunds can return money to a card, but returns policy is separate from invoice payment and monthly billing. Support agents should not confuse a refund window with invoice payment collection.

title: International Shipping Customs
body: International shipping requires customs declarations, carrier tracking, harmonized tariff codes, and delivery estimates. Domestic shipping has a simpler carrier flow. Customs forms must be attached before dispatch.

title: Webhook HMAC Signatures
body: Webhook payloads are signed with an HMAC SHA256 signature header. Consumers must verify the signature before accepting an event. Failed webhook delivery retries with exponential backoff.

title: Audit Log Exports
body: Audit logs capture administrative actions and retention can be configured up to seven years. Audit log export jobs stream NDJSON archives to S3-compatible storage. Export jobs include metadata and integrity checks.

title: Encryption Key Rotation
body: Encryption at rest uses AES-256. Keys rotate automatically every thirty days. Customer-managed encryption keys are available for enterprise tenants and require key rotation auditing.

title: Cursor Pagination
body: Cursor pagination uses opaque next page tokens instead of offsets. Each list response returns a next cursor. Pagination remains stable across concurrent writes and exports.

title: Rate Limit Retry Headers
body: Rate limits apply per API key and reset every minute. Exceeding a limit returns HTTP 429 with a retry header. Clients should back off before retrying requests.

title: SAML Authentication
body: Single sign-on authentication uses SAML metadata from the identity provider. Authentication tokens expire after twenty-four hours. This document mentions billing and export only as examples of protected resources.
